sources.writer_profile: Andy Hayler is one of the world's foremost professional restaurant critics, known for systematically reviewing three-Michelin-star establishments globally. He visited Noor in Córdoba in June 2024, shortly after it received its third Michelin star, providing a detailed independent critical assessment of chef Paco Morales's Al-Andalus tasting menu. His reviews are rigorously scored and consistently independent of commercial relationships.

Hanne Olsen is a Danish award-winning travel journalist who specialises in Spain, frequently living and working in the country. Her December 2025 guide to Córdoba's food scene reflects seven years of covering Andalucía's gastronomy, taberna culture, and the Montilla-Moriles DO, with a focus on authentic neighbourhood eating over tourist-facing venues.

Molly is a long-term British expat based in Spain since 1998, writing about Spanish food and travel under the name Piccavey. Her January 2026 Córdoba restaurant guide draws on repeated visits since 2006 and fluency in Spanish, prioritising local neighbourhood spots alongside tourist-facing tabernas. She also leads food tours in Andalucía.

Juan Velasco is a staff journalist at Cordópolis, the Córdoba edition of the national Spanish newspaper elDiario.es, covering culture, society and gastronomy in the city. His November 2025 article on Córdoba's Michelin star restaurants reports directly from the Michelin Guide awards ceremony, providing a local journalistic perspective on the city's fine-dining scene.

Alfonso Alba is a journalist at Cordópolis, the Córdoba local newspaper, covering gastronomy and society. His November 2025 article on the ReComiendo Michelin star award provides first-hand local reportage including chef Periko Ortega's own words and the historical background of the restaurant's trajectory since 2014.

sources.publication: El Debate / La Voz de Córdoba
sources.writer_profile: M. Ángeles Ramírez is a journalist with La Voz de Córdoba, the Córdoba section of the national digital newspaper El Debate, covering local gastronomy and society. Her articles from February 2026 (Repsol new additions) and December 2025 (Michelin awards roundup) represent consistent local food journalism focused on Córdoba's evolving restaurant scene.

Brenda Alonso writes about travel and food for The Objective, an independent Spanish digital publication known for long-form journalism. Her August 2023 Córdoba restaurant guide covers thirteen establishments across the city's historic neighbourhoods, with a focus on traditional taberna culture and authentic local cuisine.

Txema Aguado is one half of the travel and gastronomy blog Yendo por la Vida, a Spanish-language platform covering food, sustainable tourism and cultural events across Spain. His January 2024 review of Choco offers a detailed first-person account of both the Menú Barrio Antiguo and the Menú Kisko García, including course-by-course descriptions and a kitchen visit.

Morgane Mazelier is a French travel writer who contributes to Andalucía Lovers, a bilingual travel blog focused on southern Spain. Her November 2024 Córdoba restaurant guide covers fifteen establishments across the city, with an emphasis on neighbourhood restaurants in Santa Marina and the Judería, based on personal visits.

restaurants_raw.name: Noor
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Toasted pine nuts with green apple flavoured with ras al hanout spice mix and pine nut ice cream
restaurants_raw.quote: Noor offers some very prettily presented and quite original food in a welcoming environment, and at a price that is not excessive.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: EN primary + ES primary + EN primary + ES primary

restaurants_raw.name: Choco
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Lechuga Aliñá; Gazpachuelo frío y Quisquillas; Cigala templada y morcilla
restaurants_raw.quote: Recuerdos de su infancia en Villanueva de Córdoba y el Valle de Los Pedroches, impregnados de sal, vinagre y mantecas — the menu draws on childhood memories and regional heritage through 17 avant-garde courses.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants_raw.name: ReComiendo
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Mazamorra; four appetizers themed around daily meals (breakfast, lunch, snack, dinner); artisanal breads with 20+ olive oils
restaurants_raw.quote: Diversión, técnica, sabor, recuerdos, profesionalidad… y la personal filosofía culinaria del chef Periko Ortega, que hace todo lo posible por poner 'power' en los platos — Michelin Guide on ReComiendo's first star.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + ES primary

restaurants_raw.name: La Taberna de Almodóvar
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Berenjenas con miel; rabo de toro; croquetas
restaurants_raw.quote: Suckling lamb shoulder, croquetas with serrano ham, and tuna tataki — classic recipes with a rich Córdoban stamp and excellent value for money.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN secondary + EN primary

restaurants_raw.name: La Cuchara de San Lorenzo
name_variants: Restaurante La Cuchara de San Lorenzo
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Veal tripe; torrija caramelizada con sopa de coco; tapas and tasting menus balancing classics with market ingredients
restaurants_raw.quote: A Bib Gourmand restaurant run by two brothers — Narciso front of house and Paco in the kitchen — focusing on local and traditionally inspired cooking with lots of attention to detail and daily specials influenced by market availability.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants_raw.name: Regadera
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Ceviche de corvina; deshuesado rabo de toro con mousse de patata; creamy salmorejo with jamón serrano
restaurants_raw.quote: Fresh and modern with watering cans dotted here and there — a welcoming contemporary space on the Ronda de Isasa with chef Adrián Caballero's market-driven menu and Michelin Bib Gourmand recognition.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + EN primary + ES primary

restaurants_raw.name: El Envero
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Olla podrida frita; royal de cocido con huevas y caviar; cordero al horno con wagyu A5; sorbete de piña con anís y mascarpone
restaurants_raw.quote: Chef Manuel Valera's El Envero, in its ninth season, pays homage to the 18th century with a menu of gems including fried olla podrida, royal of stew with bottarga and caviar, and sea bass with rose escabeche.

restaurants_raw.name: Terra Olea
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Vegetable-focused dishes with aromatic broths; recovery and adaptation of old Córdoban recipes through modern technique
restaurants_raw.quote: Chef Paco Villar has based his proposal at Terra Olea on the recovery and adaptation of old recipes, in a respectful reformulation of native Córdoban cuisine — awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand and one Repsol Sol.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna La Viuda
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Rabo de toro (award-winning 2025); lemon marinated salmon with saffron spiced couscous; oxtail stew
restaurants_raw.quote: The highlight was our rabo de toro, which was an intense explosion of flavor in La Viuda's version. After we visited the restaurant, La Viuda received the award for the best rabo de toro in Córdoba 2025.

restaurants_raw.name: Casa Pepe de la Judería
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Salmorejo cordobés; rabo de toro; honey roasted eggplant; fresh tuna with local vegetables
restaurants_raw.quote: Pepe Giménez Aroca opened Casa Pepe in 1928 and Miguel Cabezas Morón modernized the restaurant — today a Córdoba institution on a corner in the winding streets of the Jewish quarter, serving tapas and Andalusian dishes in classic and modern versions.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: EN primary + ES primary + EN primary + EN secondary

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna La Montillana
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Oxtails in oloroso sauce; confit Iberian pork cheeks with foie gras; honey roasted eggplant; flamenquín
restaurants_raw.quote: Both dishes — the oxtails in oloroso sauce and confit Iberian pork cheeks with foie gras — are some of the best versions I've had in Spain.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: EN primary + EN primary + ES primary

restaurants_raw.name: El Churrasco
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Churrasco cordobés (Iberian pork sirloin grilled over encina oak charcoal with two Arabic-influenced sauces); rabo de toro; jamón de bellota
restaurants_raw.quote: Founded in 1970, El Churrasco has become a gastronomic institution in the heart of the Judería, celebrated for its meats grilled over encina oak charcoal and a wine cellar with over 900 references.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + EN primary + EN primary

restaurants_raw.name: Bodegas Campos
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Salmorejo tradicional; rabo de toro; bacalao confitado; Mojito sorbet; deep ruby red gazpacho
restaurants_raw.quote: A wine producer since 1908 housed in a carefully restored bodega — 'The deep ruby red colour of the gazpacho was like no other' — frequented by Spanish royalty and international celebrities who sign the Montilla-Moriles barrels.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna La Cazuela de la Espartería
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Flamenquín cordobés (fried sirloin rolled in serrano ham); salmorejo; stuffed mussels; small fried fish; tuna tartare
restaurants_raw.quote: Since the restaurant opened in 1998, the focus has been on local gastronomy — festively decorated with Córdoba blue and white colors, serving typical dishes including flamenquín cordobés, fried fish and salmorejo.

restaurants_raw.name: Bar Santos
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Tortilla española (made with 6kg potatoes and 20 eggs)
restaurants_raw.quote: Standing room only — this has become a must-do when visiting Córdoba for its legendary fluffy tortilla española with potato and onion, served just steps from the Mezquita.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: EN primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants_raw.name: Amaltea
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Syrian pâté; muhammara (walnut and roasted pepper pâté); tabulé; dishes from ecological garden, certified meats and sustainably caught fish
restaurants_raw.quote: A conscious and sustainable proposal on the banks of the Guadalquivir that cooks with produce from its ecological garden, certified meats and sustainably caught fish — recognised by the Repsol Guide 2026.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + EN primary + EN primary

restaurants_raw.name: Casa Salvador-Dmerkado
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Rabo de toro; tartares (tuna and steak); rice dishes; offal (casquería); seasonal vegetables with local sierra meat
restaurants_raw.quote: Un escenario de elegancia sobria donde la calidad del producto y la tranquilidad del entorno garantizan una experiencia impecable — a setting of sober elegance where product quality guarantees a flawless experience.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna El Nº 10
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Salmorejo cordobés with jamón serrano and huevo duro; croquetas de cocido; local fino and amontillado wines
restaurants_raw.quote: Taberna El Nº 10 has built a reputation for its exceptional take on Andalusian cuisine — serving salmorejo cordobés with jamón serrano and local Montilla-Moriles wines in a classic Judería setting.
restaurants_raw.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants_raw.name: Casa Rubio
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Flamenquines caseros; cordero sefardí; pastel cordobés
restaurants_raw.quote: Casa Rubio blends tradition with modernity — serving flamenquines caseros and cordero sefardí with rooftop views of the old quarter, steps from the Almódovar Gate.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna El Abanico
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Berenjenas con miel de caña (eggplant with cane honey)
restaurants_raw.quote: Located on Plaza de San Miguel, Taberna El Abanico is celebrated for its berenjenas con miel de caña — the definitive Córdoban signature dish, cane honey-drizzled fried eggplant.

restaurants_raw.name: La Boca
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Three-course vegetarian daily menu; zero-kilometre products; wood-fired preparations
restaurants_raw.quote: A welcoming café and restaurant on Calle San Fernando offering a three-course vegetarian daily menu alongside meat options, with loyal customers for over a decade.

restaurants_raw.name: Garum 2.1
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Salmorejo de mango con langostinos; octopus with fried pig ears over creamy potato purée; rabo de toro; torrija
restaurants_raw.quote: Award-winning tapas bar at the foot of El Brillante offering modern reinterpretations of Andalucian classics — the octopus with fried pig ears over creamy potato is an unexpected Córdoba specialty.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna Góngora
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Flamenquines de Córdoba; salmorejo; grilled octopus; croquetas
restaurants_raw.quote: Located near the Mosque-Cathedral, Taberna Góngora serves classic flamenquines de Córdoba and salmorejo at affordable prices in an unpretentious neighbourhood setting.

restaurants_raw.name: Mercado Victoria
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Galician octopus; salmorejo; black-footed Córdoba ham; ice cream 'La Mezquita'; paella; bastela
restaurants_raw.quote: In the middle of Córdoba's green lung, the Jardínes de la Victoria, lies Córdoba's modern gastronomy market — over 20 different stalls with typical tapas from Córdoba and other parts of the world.

restaurants_raw.name: Salón de Té
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Tagine; couscous; ziryab eggplant; hummus; falafel; kibbeh; baklava; Andalusian and Arabic teas
restaurants_raw.quote: Set in a courtyard decorated with colorful tiles, rugs and cushions — an oasis of calm in the centre of Córdoba, serving Arabic and Andalusian dishes and a large selection of homemade teas.

restaurants_raw.name: La Taberna del Río
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Salmorejo; local cheeses from Pedroches Valley; Montilla-Moriles wines
restaurants_raw.quote: Highly recommended for typical local Córdoban delicacies — salmorejo, local Pedroches cheese, and Montilla-Moriles wine served riverside on the Paseo de la Ribera.

restaurants_raw.name: La Maceta
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Argentinian burger with chimichurri and crispy onions; ensalada russa; sirloin with tomato, mushrooms and caramelized onions
restaurants_raw.quote: A small, bright and modern tapas bar on Plaza del Escudo with over 40 tapas on the menu, including both classic Córdoban and contemporary flavors.

restaurants_raw.name: La Ermita de la Candelaria
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Pisto; mazamorra con aire de Almodóvar del Río; sorbete de naranja fresca con AOVE; tostas de steak tartar
restaurants_raw.quote: Chef Javier Campos transformed a 15th-century hermitage into a gastronomic reference in Córdoba — committed to 'la cocina de siempre, comprometida con los productos de nuestra tierra', awarded one Sol Repsol in 2024.

restaurants_raw.name: La Casa de Manolete Bistró
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Beet salmorejo with tuna; creative mazamorra; iberian pork and game preparations
restaurants_raw.quote: An elegant bistronómic restaurant housed in the reformed palace where bullfighter Manolete and Ortega y Gasset once lived — chefs Juanjo Ruiz and Reme Romero awarded one Sol Repsol.

restaurants_raw.name: Bodegas Mezquita Céspedes
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Remojón cordobés; bacalao confitado en AOVE con pisto cordobés; tajín de cordero sefardí; rabo de toro a la cordobesa; carrillera ibérica; ensaladilla de atún y pulpo con crujiente de arroz negro
restaurants_raw.quote: Named Spain's best casual restaurant in 2024 and third-best worldwide in its category — a menú del día at €20.60 featuring remojón cordobés, bacalao confitado, and rabo de toro a la cordobesa.

restaurants_raw.name: La Taberna de Almodóvar
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Suckling lamb shoulder; croquetas with serrano ham; tuna tataki; berenjenas con miel
restaurants_raw.quote: A Bib Gourmand restaurant in the Santa Marina neighbourhood offering classic recipes with a rich Córdoban stamp — the suckling lamb shoulder is a standout alongside croquetas with serrano ham.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna San Miguel Casa El Pisto
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Pisto con huevo frito; pescaito frito; iberian ham; traditional stews
restaurants_raw.quote: Operating since 1880, this authentic tavern on Plaza de San Miguel displays classic Córdoban décor and serves traditional dishes including pisto con huevo frito and pescaito frito at very moderate prices.

restaurants_raw.name: Los Berengueles
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Salmorejo; rabo de toro; flamenquines; ensaladilla rusa (made fresh daily); berenjenas con miel
restaurants_raw.quote: A multi-generational family establishment near Calle Conde de Torres Cabrera emphasizing product quality — the ensaladilla rusa is made fresh daily and the rabo de toro is one of the better versions in the city.

restaurants_raw.name: La Tinaja
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Aubergines with Honey; Potato Pots with brava sauce; Cod-filled ravioli
restaurants_raw.quote: Service here was excellent and the atmosphere inside was relaxed and welcoming — a riverside restaurant offering three courses for around €28, with aubergines with honey and cod-filled ravioli among the highlights.

restaurants_raw.name: El Astronauta
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Moussaka vegana; salmorejo con aguacate; healthy midweek menu del día at €10
restaurants_raw.quote: A cool restaurant below the Roman Temple with healthy options and vegetarian dishes — the €10 midweek menu and half-portion options make it a local favourite for affordable creative eating.

restaurants_raw.name: La Lianta de Quini
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Almond mazamorra with apple and sardines; oxtail canelones
restaurants_raw.quote: A creative contemporary taberna in the Judería neighbourhood serving almond mazamorra with apple and sardines and oxtail canelones — inventive updates on Córdoban classics.
